2.solution: Let u=x+1/x. x²+x+1/x²+1/x=(x+1/x)²−2+(x+1/x)=u²+u−2=(u+2)(u−1)<0 requires −2<u<1. This means BOTH u>−2 (option A) AND u<1 (option C) must hold. Answer: D (Both A and C).

3.solution: Case x≥1: |x−1|=x−1, inside=x−(x−1)+x+3=x+4. |x+4|<4 → x<0; contradicts x≥1 → no solution. Case x<1: |x−1|=1−x, inside=x−(1−x)+x+3=3x+2. |3x+2|<4 → −2<x<2/3. Intersect with x<1: −2<x<2/3. Integers: {−1,0}=2 values.

5.solution: Let k=y²−5y+3. Need kx²+(k−2)x+k<0 for all x∈R. Requires k<0 AND discriminant<0. Disc=(k−2)²−4k²=−3k²−4k+4<0 → 3k²+4k−4>0 → k<−2 or k>2/3. Combined with k<0: k<−2. So y²−5y+3<−2 → y²−5y+5<0 → y∈((5−√5)/2,(5+√5)/2). Answer: D.

6.solution: Roots p,q: p+q=−(a+3), pq=−(a+5). p²+q²=(p+q)²−2pq=(a+3)²+2(a+5)=a²+8a+19=(a+4)²+3. Minimum=3 at a=−4. Check: disc=(a+3)²+4(a+5)=a²+10a+29=(a+5)²+4>0 always. Answer: 3.

10.solution: 2ⁿ(n+1)ⁿ/nⁿ=2ⁿ×(1+1/n)ⁿ. Lower: (1+1/n)ⁿ≥2 for all n≥1 (equality at n=1) → expression≥2·2ⁿ=2^(n+1). Upper: (1+1/n)ⁿ<e<3 → expression<3·2ⁿ. So 2^(n+1)≤2ⁿ(n+1)ⁿ/nⁿ≤3·2ⁿ. Answer: B.
